title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked authentic Chinese Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao) with a golden, crispy topping.
seo.imageCaption: Master the art of making these beloved Hong Kong-style Pineapple Buns, featuring a signature crunchy, sweet crust and a fluffy interior.

description: Pineapple Buns, or Bo Lo Bao, are a quintessential Hong Kong bakery classic, beloved for their unique contrast of a sweet, crumbly, cookie-like topping and a soft, fluffy bread base. Despite their name, these buns contain no pineapple, but rather get their name from the signature crisscross pattern on the topping that resembles a pineapple skin. Enjoy these warm from the oven for a comforting treat.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine both flours, sugar, yeast, and salt. Add the egg and lukewarm milk. Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Turn it out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for about 10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Incorporate the softened butter and knead for another 5-7 minutes until the dough is smooth and passes the windowpane test.

instructions.title: First Proofing
instructions.text: Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, turning once to coat. Cover with plastic wrap and let it proof in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until it has doubled in size. This step is crucial for a soft bun texture.

instructions.title: Make the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: While the dough is proofing, prepare the topping. In a separate bowl, cream together the softened butter and powdered sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the egg yolk and milk powder until well combined. Gradually add the all-purpose flour and baking powder, mixing until a soft, pliable dough forms. Divide this topping dough into 8 equal portions.

instructions.title: Shape the Buns
instructions.text: Once the dough has doubled, punch it down gently to release the air. Divide the dough into 8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball. On a lightly floured surface, flatten each topping portion into a disc large enough to cover about two-thirds of the bun. Place a topping disc over each dough ball.

instructions.title: Score and Second Proofing
instructions.text: Gently press the topping onto the bun. Using a dough scraper or knife, lightly score a crisscross pattern on the topping to create the pineapple effect. Be careful not to cut too deep into the bread dough. Arrange the buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let them proof again in a warm place for 30-45 minutes, or until noticeably puffy.

instructions.title: Bake the Pineapple Buns
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Brush the tops of the scored buns with the egg wash. Bake for 15-18 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and crispy, and the buns are cooked through. The sweet aroma will fill your kitchen.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, transfer the Pineapple Buns to a wire rack to cool slightly. They are best enjoyed warm, ideally within a few hours of baking to appreciate the crispy topping and soft interior.

tips: For an extra crispy topping, ensure your butter for the topping is softened but not melted. Work quickly to prevent it from becoming too warm.
If you want to add a savory twist, you can insert a small piece of butter or a slice of char siu (BBQ pork) into the center of the dough ball before adding the topping.
Pineapple Buns are best eaten fresh. If you have leftovers, reheat them briefly in a toaster oven or microwave to regain some of their warmth and softness.
article:
Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao): A Taste of Hong Kong's Beloved Pastry

There are few pastries in the world that evoke the same comforting nostalgia and immediate delight as the Pineapple Bun, known in Cantonese as Bo Lo Bao. This iconic Chinese pastry, a staple in bakeries across Hong Kong and beyond, holds a special place in the hearts of many. With its distinctive crispy, crumbly, and sweet topping contrasting beautifully with a soft, fluffy bun underneath, it’s a textural masterpiece that offers a unique culinary experience. Despite its name, the Pineapple Bun contains no actual pineapple; its moniker comes from the crackled, golden-brown topping that resembles the skin of a pineapple. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that bakery-fresh magic at home, you're in the right place. This article will guide you through everything you need to know about Bo Lo Bao, including a comprehensive Pineapple Bun Recipe.

## What is Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao)?

Bo Lo Bao, or Pineapple Bun, is a classic sweet bun originating from Hong Kong. Its defining characteristic is a rich, sugary crust that forms on top during baking, often made with a mixture of sugar, eggs, flour, and butter. This crust bakes into a delightful mosaic of cracks, giving it the signature pineapple-like appearance and a satisfying crunch with every bite. Beneath this crisp exterior lies a supremely soft, slightly sweet bread dough, reminiscent of Japanese milk bread in its tenderness.

The Pineapple Bun is a breakfast favorite, an afternoon snack, and a beloved dim sum item. It’s often enjoyed plain, but the most popular way to eat it is "Bo Lo Yau" (菠蘿油), where a thick slab of cold butter is inserted into a warm bun, creating a sublime contrast of temperatures and textures. The melting butter adds a creamy, salty counterpoint to the bun's sweetness, making for an irresistibly decadent treat. Its popularity has spread globally, making it a must-try for anyone exploring Chinese baking.

## Ingredients for Perfect Pineapple Buns

Crafting the perfect Pineapple Bun involves two main components: the soft bread dough and the signature crispy topping. Precision in ingredients and measurements is key to achieving that authentic taste and texture. For a reliable Pineapple Bun Recipe, here's what you'll need:

For the Bun Dough:
*   **All-purpose flour or bread flour:** Provides the structure. Bread flour offers a chewier texture.
*   **Granulated sugar:** Sweetens the dough and aids in browning.
*   **Instant dry yeast:** The leavening agent for a fluffy bun.
*   **Salt:** Balances the flavors.
*   **Milk:** Adds richness and moisture. Whole milk is recommended.
*   **Egg:** Contributes to richness, color, and tenderness.
*   **Unsalted butter:** Makes the dough tender and adds flavor.

For the Pineapple Topping:
*   **Unsalted butter:** The base for the rich, crumbly topping.
*   **Powdered sugar (or granulated sugar):** For sweetness and fine texture.
*   **Egg yolk (or whole egg):** Binds the topping and adds color.
*   **All-purpose flour:** Gives structure to the crust.
*   **Milk powder (optional):** Enhances flavor and richness.
*   **Baking powder (optional):** Helps create a crispier, more crackled texture.
*   **Egg wash (for brushing before baking):** For a golden sheen.

Ensure all ingredients are at room temperature, especially butter and eggs, for easier mixing and better dough development. This detailed list forms the foundation of any great Pineapple Bun Recipe.

## Step-by-Step Guide to Making Bo Lo Bao

Making Bo Lo Bao at home is a rewarding process that, while requiring a bit of time and patience, is entirely achievable. Follow this step-by-step Pineapple Bun Recipe for delicious results:

1.  **Prepare the Bun Dough:**
    *   In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, yeast, and salt.
    *   Add milk and egg, mixing until a shaggy dough forms.
    *   Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 10-15 minutes until smooth and elastic. Alternatively, use a stand mixer with a dough hook.
    *   Incorporate the softened butter and continue kneading until the butter is fully absorbed and the dough is very smooth and passes the "windowpane test" (stretches thin without tearing).
    *   Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let it proof in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

2.  **Prepare the Pineapple Topping:**
    *   While the dough is proofing, cream together softened butter and powdered sugar until light and fluffy.
    *   Beat in the egg yolk until well combined.
    *   Gradually mix in the flour, milk powder (if using), and baking powder (if using) until a soft, uniform dough forms. Avoid overmixing.
    *   Wrap the topping dough in plastic wrap and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes. This makes it easier to handle.

3.  **Assemble the Buns:**
    *   Once the bun dough has doubled, punch it down gently and divide it into 10-12 equal pieces.
    *   Shape each piece into a smooth ball. Place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them.
    *   Take the chilled topping dough and divide it into the same number of pieces as the bun dough.
    *   Flatten each piece of topping dough into a thin disc, roughly 3-4 inches in diameter. You can do this by placing it between two sheets of plastic wrap or lightly flouring your surface.
    *   Carefully place a topping disc over each bun dough ball, gently pressing it down to adhere. The topping should cover about two-thirds of the bun's surface. You can lightly score a crisscross pattern on the topping with a knife for a more pronounced pineapple pattern.

4.  **Second Proofing and Baking:**
    *   Cover the assembled buns loosely with plastic wrap and let them proof again in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.
    *   Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C).
    *   Just before baking, gently brush the exposed topping with egg wash (a beaten egg).
    *   Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and crackled, and the buns are cooked through.
    *   Transfer the baked buns to a wire rack to cool slightly.

## Tips for a Crispy Topping and Soft Bun

Achieving the perfect balance of a crunchy, sweet topping and a soft, airy bun is the hallmark of a great Pineapple Bun. Here are some pro tips to perfect your Pineapple Bun Recipe:

*   **For the Crispy Topping:**
    *   **Don't overmix:** Overmixing the topping can develop gluten, making it tough instead of crumbly. Mix just until combined.
    *   **Chill the topping:** Chilling helps the butter solidify, making the topping easier to handle and less likely to melt prematurely in the oven, thus promoting better crackling.
    *   **Score lightly:** A light crisscross score on the topping before baking encourages those beautiful, characteristic cracks.
    *   **High initial heat:** Some bakers advocate for starting with a slightly higher oven temperature for the first 5-7 minutes to help the topping set and crisp up quickly, then reducing it to finish baking the bun.
*   **For the Soft Bun:**
    *   **Proper kneading:** Ensure your dough is well-kneaded until it's very elastic and smooth. This develops the gluten necessary for a soft, chewy texture.
    *   **Adequate proofing:** Don't rush the proofing stages. The dough needs time to rise fully to become light and airy. An under-proofed bun will be dense, while an over-proofed bun might collapse.
    *   **Don't overbake:** Overbaking will dry out the buns. Keep an eye on them and remove them from the oven as soon as they are golden brown and cooked through.

## Serving Suggestions and Pairings

The versatility of the Pineapple Bun makes it suitable for various occasions. While delicious on its own, certain pairings elevate the experience:

*   **Bo Lo Yau (Pineapple Bun with Butter):** The absolute classic. Slice a warm bun horizontally and insert a thick, cold slice of unsalted butter. The contrast of the warm bun and melting butter is pure bliss.
*   **With Coffee or Hong Kong-style Milk Tea:** The perfect breakfast or afternoon tea companion. The slight bitterness of the drinks cuts through the bun's sweetness beautifully.
*   **Soy Milk:** A lighter, traditional pairing that's both nutritious and comforting.
*   **Plain:** Sometimes, simplicity is best. Enjoy the intricate textures and flavors of the bun as is.
*   **As part of a Dim Sum Spread:** It complements savory dim sum dishes, offering a sweet balance to the meal.

## Variations: Sweet & Savory Fillings

While the traditional Pineapple Bun is usually unfilled, its popularity has led to creative variations incorporating both sweet and savory fillings. These adaptations build upon the basic Pineapple Bun Recipe, adding an exciting layer of flavor.

*   **Sweet Fillings:**
    *   **Custard:** A rich, creamy egg custard filling is a popular choice, adding another layer of sweetness and moisture.
    *   **Red Bean Paste:** A sweet, earthy filling made from mashed red beans, common in many Asian desserts.
    *   **Coconut:** A sweet, shredded coconut filling can add a tropical twist.
*   **Savory Fillings:**
    *   **BBQ Pork (Char Siu):** A beloved savory option, filling the bun with tender, sweet, and savory roasted pork. This creates a delightful contrast with the sweet topping.
    *   **Ham and Egg:** A simple yet satisfying breakfast-style filling, often featuring scrambled egg and diced ham.

To incorporate fillings, simply flatten each dough ball after the first proofing, place a spoonful of your chosen filling in the center, and then pinch the dough to seal it completely before shaping it back into a ball. Then proceed with applying the topping and baking as usual. Experimenting with these variations can lead to your new favorite version of this classic Chinese pastry, proving the adaptability of the core Pineapple Bun Recipe.

The Pineapple Bun is more than just a pastry; it's a cultural icon, a testament to the ingenuity and deliciousness of Chinese baking. Its unique combination of textures and flavors makes it unforgettable. Whether you choose to enjoy it plain, with butter, or experiment with fillings, mastering this Pineapple Bun Recipe at home will bring a piece of Hong Kong's culinary heart into your kitchen. So, roll up your sleeves, gather your ingredients, and embark on a delicious journey to bake your very own perfect Bo Lo Bao.
